title: Implement Rust SDK core API: channels, conversations, and type enforcement
purpose: Deliver the core application-facing API surface of the Rust SDK: ChannelHandle for typed publish/subscribe/request-reply (ADR-002), ConversationHandle for supervised message exchange (ADR-001), and the SchemaValidate trait for compile-time type enforcement. These types define the contract every deployment mode (embedded, remote) must satisfy.
task: Create channel.rs, conversation.rs, and types.rs in crates/liminal-sdk/src/. ChannelHandle is a trait with generic methods constrained by Serialize+SchemaValidate (publish) and Deserialize (subscribe). ConversationHandle is a trait with send/receive/lifecycle observation. SchemaValidate is a trait that message types implement to provide schema metadata for publish-time validation. All types are trait definitions at this stage -- concrete implementations come in SDK-004 (embedded/remote modes). IN SCOPE: trait definitions, type constraints, associated types, error integration with SdkError. OUT OF SCOPE: concrete implementations, connection management, backpressure types, embedded/remote distinction.

requirements.title: Define ChannelHandle trait with typed publish, subscribe, and request-reply
requirements.spec: WHEN a caller invokes publish on a ChannelHandle, THE SYSTEM SHALL require the message type to implement Serialize + SchemaValidate. WHEN a caller invokes subscribe, THE SYSTEM SHALL return a stream of messages whose type implements Deserialize. WHEN a caller invokes request_reply, THE SYSTEM SHALL send a message and return a future resolving to the reply. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose protocol framing or wire details through the ChannelHandle interface.
acceptance: ChannelHandle::publish<M: Serialize + SchemaValidate> compiles and accepts a typed message
ChannelHandle::subscribe returns an impl Stream<Item = Result<M, SdkError>> where M: Deserialize
ChannelHandle::request_reply<Req, Resp> sends Req and returns Future<Output = Result<Resp, SdkError>>
Attempting to publish a type not implementing SchemaValidate produces a compile-time error
ChannelHandle is object-safe or documents why it is not and provides an alternative

files: docs/design/sdk/briefs/SDK-002.json:14-31 — Exact ChannelHandle requirement, acceptance criteria, and boundaries for typed publish/subscribe/request_reply.
docs/design/sdk/design.json:70-100 — SDK structure declares `crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs` and `types.rs` as the intended files for SDK-002.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:43-146 — Existing core `ChannelHandle` is a concrete byte-oriented type; read it only to avoid accidentally copying its raw payload API into the SDK trait.
crates/liminal/src/channel/subscription.rs:10-40 — Core subscription yields envelopes through `try_next`; SDK subscribe must instead expose typed stream items and hide envelopes/wire details.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — Workspace lints deny unsafe, unwrap, expect, panic, and all clippy warnings; new trait definitions must be lint-clean.
context: `crates/liminal-sdk` is absent in this checkout; SDK-001’s brief says it should have created `Cargo.toml`, `src/lib.rs`, and `src/error.rs`, so apply or recreate that scaffold before adding `channel.rs`.

The core crate already exports a concrete `liminal::ChannelHandle`, but it accepts `Payload: AsRef<[u8]>` and returns `Result<(), LiminalError>`; SDK-002 requires a distinct `liminal_sdk::channel::ChannelHandle` trait that is typed and does not expose bytes, envelopes, protocol frames, or core actor details.

Trait methods with generic type parameters are not object-safe. The acceptance explicitly allows documenting why `ChannelHandle` is not object-safe and providing an alternative; the cleanest alternative is usually an associated-type-based boxed stream/future surface plus docs explaining implementers use concrete handle types or enum wrappers until object-safe erased APIs are introduced.

The brief mentions future backpressure but SDK-003 owns response types, so for this brief `publish` should return `Result<(), SdkError>` or a documented placeholder consistent with SDK-001, not invent `Accept/Defer/Reject`.
scout.approach: Create `crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs` defining a documented `pub trait ChannelHandle: core::fmt::Debug + Send + Sync` (if no_std goals are already in place, prefer `core::fmt::Debug`; otherwise it still works under std). Use `serde::Serialize`, `serde::de::DeserializeOwned`, and `crate::{SdkError, types::SchemaValidate}` bounds. Because stable Rust traits cannot return `impl Stream` or `impl Future` in a broadly object-safe way and async trait generics are not object-safe, define associated generic return types if using GATs (for example `type Subscription<M>: futures_core::Stream<Item = Result<M, SdkError>> where M: DeserializeOwned;` and `type ReplyFuture<'a, Resp>: core::future::Future<Output = Result<Resp, SdkError>> + 'a where Self: 'a, Resp: DeserializeOwned + 'a;`) or boxed aliases if the SDK manifest already permits `alloc`. Then implement `publish<M>(&self, message: M) -> Result<(), SdkError> where M: Serialize + SchemaValidate`, `subscribe<M>(&self) -> Result<Self::Subscription<M>, SdkError> where M: DeserializeOwned`, and `request_reply<Req, Resp>(&self, request: Req) -> Self::ReplyFuture<'_, Resp> where Req: Serialize + SchemaValidate, Resp: DeserializeOwned`. Add rustdoc on the trait explaining non-object-safety caused by generic methods/GATs and indicating that concrete handle types (or a later erased wrapper) are the intended alternative.
scout.notes: Do not copy the core crate’s `publish<Payload: AsRef<[u8]>>` shape: that would violate both ADR-002 and R4. If `futures_core` is not already in `liminal-sdk/Cargo.toml`, add the smallest no_std-compatible dependency (`futures-core` with default features disabled if needed) rather than a runtime such as tokio; no network/runtime dependency belongs in this brief.

files_changed.note: Added `crates/liminal-sdk` to workspace members so SDK package verification can run.
Recorded the new `liminal-sdk` package and its no_std-compatible dependencies.
Created the SDK manifest with `serde`, `futures-core`, and `thiserror` dependencies; no network dependencies were added.
Created the crate root and re-exported `ChannelHandle`.
Created `SdkError` so channel API methods can return SDK-owned errors.
Defined the typed `ChannelHandle` trait and associated stream/future return types.

dev.how: `liminal_sdk::channel::ChannelHandle` now requires `Debug + Send + Sync`, exposes `publish`, `subscribe`, and `request_reply`, and hides all wire/protocol/envelope details. `publish<M>` and request `Req` require `Serialize + SchemaValidate`; `subscribe<M>` and reply `Resp` use `DeserializeOwned`; subscription and reply return types are associated generic stream/future types. Rustdoc documents why the trait is intentionally not object-safe and points callers to concrete handle generic bounds or application enums as the alternative.

checklist.note: `ChannelHandle` is defined in `crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs` and re-exported from the crate root.

stories.note: The trait provides a typed in-process/embedded-compatible API surface for zero-hop implementations without exposing core raw-payload handles.

This brief intentionally does not define SDK-003 backpressure response types; `publish` returns `Result<(), SdkError>`, so future concrete implementations can surface pressure through `SdkError::Backpressure` until the typed response vocabulary lands.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelHandle::publish<M: Serialize + SchemaValidate> compiles and accepts a typed message
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:64-66 defines publish<M>(&self, message: M) -> Result<(), SdkError> where M: Serialize + SchemaValidate; cargo check -p liminal-sdk exited 0 after hardening.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelHandle::subscribe returns an impl Stream<Item = Result<M, SdkError>> where M: Deserialize
acceptance.evidence: Fixed in harden pass: crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:45-47 defines Subscription<M>: Stream<Item = Result<M, SdkError>> where M: DeserializeOwned, and crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:72-74 now returns Self::Subscription<M> directly.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelHandle::request_reply<Req, Resp> sends Req and returns Future<Output = Result<Resp, SdkError>>
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:50-53 defines ReplyFuture<'a, Resp>: Future<Output = Result<Resp, SdkError>>; crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:87-90 accepts request: Req with Req: Serialize + SchemaValidate and Resp: DeserializeOwned.

acceptance.criterion: Attempting to publish a type not implementing SchemaValidate produces a compile-time error
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:27-42 contains a compile_fail doctest publishing an only-Serialize type; cargo test -p liminal-sdk reports `test crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs - channel::ChannelHandle (line 27) - compile fail ... ok`.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelHandle is object-safe or documents why it is not and provides an alternative
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:18-25 documents that ChannelHandle is not object-safe because of generic methods and generic associated return types, and recommends concrete generic bounds or application enums as alternatives.

issues: Original implementation returned Result<Self::Subscription<M>, SdkError> from subscribe, which did not meet the direct stream return required by R1 acceptance.
Dev record claims S4 satisfied, but the actual code does not return typed Accept/Defer/Reject backpressure signals; the brief boundary explicitly defers backpressure response types to SDK-003.
fixes: Changed ChannelHandle::subscribe to return Self::Subscription<M> directly and documented that subscription/delivery failures are emitted as SdkError stream items at crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:68-74.

requirements.title: Define ConversationHandle trait with send, receive, and lifecycle observation
requirements.spec: WHEN a caller invokes send on a ConversationHandle, THE SYSTEM SHALL transmit a typed message within the conversation context. WHEN a caller invokes receive, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the next message in the conversation. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide lifecycle observation (opened, message, closing, closed, error) as a stream of ConversationEvent variants. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require callers to manage conversation process identifiers directly.
acceptance: ConversationHandle::send<M: Serialize> accepts a typed message and returns Result<(), SdkError>
ConversationHandle::receive<M: Deserialize> returns Future<Output = Result<M, SdkError>>
ConversationHandle::lifecycle returns an impl Stream<Item = ConversationEvent>
ConversationEvent enum has variants: Opened, Message, Closing, Closed, Error
Each ConversationEvent variant carries a conversation identifier

files: docs/design/sdk/briefs/SDK-002.json:32-50 — Exact ConversationHandle and ConversationEvent acceptance criteria.
docs/design/sdk/design.json:6-19 — Design states conversations/messages are application concepts and protocol/backpressure details must not leak.
docs/design/sdk/design.json:87-99 — Structure places `ConversationHandle` in `crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs` and `SchemaValidate` in `types.rs`.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:3-47 — Core conversation names and trace-context methods; useful context for identifier naming but too implementation-oriented for the SDK API.
crates/liminal/src/error.rs:24-30 — Core conversation failure variants provide vocabulary to map into SDK errors, without exposing `LiminalError` directly.
context: SDK conversation events must have exactly the lifecycle variants `Opened`, `Message`, `Closing`, `Closed`, and `Error`, and each variant must carry a conversation identifier.
Core `Conversation` currently models tracing spans and conversation names, not an SDK handle; avoid requiring callers to manage beamr process IDs or trace contexts in the SDK API.

All public types must implement `Debug`; that includes `ConversationEvent`, any `ConversationId` newtype/type alias wrapper, and the `ConversationHandle` trait bound itself if the pattern used for `ChannelHandle` includes `Debug`.
`receive<M>` should use `DeserializeOwned` rather than borrowed `Deserialize<'a>` for the same ownership reason as channel subscription: SDK users receive messages independent of transport buffers.
scout.approach: Create `crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs` with a small public identifier type and event enum before the trait. Prefer a no_std-friendly `ConversationId` newtype around `alloc::string::String` if SDK-001 already set up `extern crate alloc`; otherwise use `String` consistently with existing core naming. Define `#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)] pub enum ConversationEvent { Opened { conversation_id: ConversationId }, Message { conversation_id: ConversationId }, Closing { conversation_id: ConversationId }, Closed { conversation_id: ConversationId }, Error { conversation_id: ConversationId, error: SdkError } }` if `SdkError` is clone-compatible; if not, use an error description field or omit `Clone` rather than forcing `SdkError` changes. Define `pub trait ConversationHandle: core::fmt::Debug + Send + Sync` with `send<M>(&self, message: M) -> Result<(), SdkError> where M: Serialize`, `receive<M>(&self) -> Self::ReceiveFuture<'_, M> where M: DeserializeOwned`, and `lifecycle(&self) -> Self::LifecycleStream`, using the same associated future/stream or boxed alias convention chosen in `channel.rs`.
scout.notes: Be careful with deriving `Clone`/`PartialEq` for `ConversationEvent::Error`: SDK-001’s `SdkError` may not be cloneable or comparable. The acceptance only requires `Debug` and variants carrying identifiers, so do not over-constrain the enum if it would force churn in `SdkError`.

files_changed.note: Provided `SdkError` used by conversation operations and lifecycle error events.
Defined conversation identifier, lifecycle event enum, and typed conversation handle trait.

dev.how: `liminal_sdk::conversation` now contains a `ConversationId` newtype, a `ConversationEvent` enum with exactly `Opened`, `Message`, `Closing`, `Closed`, and `Error` variants, and a `ConversationHandle` trait. Every event variant carries a `conversation_id`; the error event also carries `SdkError`. The trait provides typed `send<M: Serialize>`, typed async `receive<M: DeserializeOwned>`, and lifecycle stream observation without any process identifier or transport exposure.

checklist.note: `ConversationHandle` is defined in `crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs` with send, receive, and lifecycle observation methods.

acceptance.criterion: ConversationHandle::send<M: Serialize> accepts a typed message and returns Result<(), SdkError>
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs:103-105 defines send<M>(&self, message: M) -> Result<(), SdkError> where M: Serialize.

acceptance.criterion: ConversationHandle::receive<M: Deserialize> returns Future<Output = Result<M, SdkError>>
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs:89-92 defines ReceiveFuture<'a, M>: Future<Output = Result<M, SdkError>> where M: DeserializeOwned; crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs:116-118 returns Self::ReceiveFuture<'_, M>.

acceptance.criterion: ConversationHandle::lifecycle returns an impl Stream<Item = ConversationEvent>
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs:94-95 defines LifecycleStream: Stream<Item = ConversationEvent>; crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs:120-121 returns Self::LifecycleStream.

acceptance.criterion: ConversationEvent enum has variants: Opened, Message, Closing, Closed, Error
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs:51-79 defines ConversationEvent with Opened, Message, Closing, Closed, and Error variants.

acceptance.criterion: Each ConversationEvent variant carries a conversation identifier
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs:54-78 shows every ConversationEvent variant includes a conversation_id: ConversationId field.

requirements.title: Define SchemaValidate trait for compile-time type enforcement
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a SchemaValidate trait that message types implement to declare their schema. The trait SHALL provide a method returning schema metadata (name, version, and schema bytes). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT perform runtime schema validation in this trait -- it provides the metadata that the bus uses for publish-time validation per ADR-002.
acceptance: SchemaValidate trait is defined in types.rs with a schema_metadata() method
schema_metadata() returns a struct containing schema name, version, and schema bytes
The trait has a blanket or derive-macro path documented for user types
SchemaValidate is re-exported from the crate root

files: docs/design/sdk/briefs/SDK-002.json:51-68 — Exact SchemaValidate acceptance criteria and explicit prohibition on runtime validation in the trait.
docs/design/sdk/design.json:20-31 — Type-safety principle and cross-SDK parity context for schema metadata.
docs/design/sdk/design.json:238-270 — Constraints include no_std+alloc, public Debug, and no silent backpressure; these affect metadata ownership and exported API shape.
crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:38-109 — Core runtime JSON Schema validation implementation; SDK `SchemaValidate` should provide metadata for this layer, not duplicate validation.
crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:199-229 — Existing schema validation error style if `SdkError::TypeValidation` later maps schema failures.
context: `SchemaValidate` belongs in `crates/liminal-sdk/src/types.rs` and must be re-exported from the crate root, so `lib.rs` from SDK-001 needs `pub mod types;` and `pub use types::{SchemaMetadata, SchemaValidate};` or equivalent.
The trait is metadata-only: do not call `jsonschema`, parse JSON, validate payloads, or introduce any runtime validation implementation in `types.rs`.

For no_std + alloc, avoid `std::borrow::Cow` and prefer `alloc::borrow::Cow` (or static byte slices) if the SDK crate is configured no_std. Schema bytes can be `&'static [u8]` for derive/static paths or `Cow<'static, [u8]>` if generated schemas may be owned.

The acceptance asks for a blanket or derive-macro path documented, not necessarily implemented. A blanket impl is hard to make meaningful without a marker/type registry and could weaken enforcement; rustdoc documenting a future `#[derive(SchemaValidate)]` and showing manual impls is safer for this brief.
scout.approach: Create `crates/liminal-sdk/src/types.rs` with a documented `#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)] pub struct SchemaMetadata` containing `name`, `version`, and `schema` fields. Use owned/borrowed types that fit no_std+alloc, e.g. `alloc::borrow::Cow<'static, str>` for name/version and `alloc::borrow::Cow<'static, [u8]>` for schema bytes; if SDK-001 did not configure no_std yet, still import through `alloc` once `extern crate alloc` is in `lib.rs`. Define `pub trait SchemaValidate { fn schema_metadata() -> SchemaMetadata; }` as an associated function so publish can get metadata without needing a value borrow. Add rustdoc with a manual impl example and a note that a derive macro will implement the same trait for generated/user message types; do not add a blanket impl for all `Serialize` because that would erase the compile-time enforcement requirement.
scout.notes: If `SchemaMetadata` stores `String`/`Vec<u8>`, it will work but makes every call allocate; `Cow<'static, _>` lets generated implementations return static schema bytes and is friendlier for WASM/no_std. Also ensure `SchemaValidate` itself is re-exported from `lib.rs`; otherwise R3 fails even if `types.rs` compiles.

files_changed.note: Re-exported `SchemaMetadata` and `SchemaValidate` from the crate root.
Defined metadata-only schema declaration types for message type enforcement.
Configured `serde` with `alloc` and disabled defaults for no_std-friendly message trait usage.

dev.how: `types.rs` now defines `SchemaMetadata` with schema `name`, `version`, and `schema` byte fields using `alloc::borrow::Cow<'static, _>` to support static generated schemas or owned values in `no_std + alloc`. `SchemaValidate::schema_metadata()` is an associated function returning that struct and performs no runtime validation. Rustdoc documents manual implementations and the future derive-macro path, while deliberately avoiding a blanket impl that would weaken compile-time enforcement.

checklist.note: `SchemaValidate` is defined in `liminal_sdk::types` and re-exported as `liminal_sdk::SchemaValidate`.

acceptance.criterion: SchemaValidate trait is defined in types.rs with a schema_metadata() method
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/types.rs:68-71 defines pub trait SchemaValidate with fn schema_metadata() -> SchemaMetadata.

acceptance.criterion: schema_metadata() returns a struct containing schema name, version, and schema bytes
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/types.rs:9-17 defines SchemaMetadata with pub name, pub version, and pub schema byte fields; crates/liminal-sdk/src/types.rs:71 returns SchemaMetadata.

acceptance.criterion: The trait has a blanket or derive-macro path documented for user types
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/types.rs:42-67 documents manual implementation today and a future #[derive(SchemaValidate)] macro path, while intentionally avoiding a blanket impl that would weaken enforcement.

acceptance.criterion: SchemaValidate is re-exported from the crate root
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/lib.rs:13 re-exports SchemaMetadata and SchemaValidate from the crate root.

requirements.title: Enforce type parameter constraints on publish and subscribe
requirements.spec: WHEN a publish method is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL require the type parameter to satisfy both Serialize and SchemaValidate bounds. WHEN a subscribe method is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL require the type parameter to satisfy DeserializeOwned. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow untyped publish or subscribe operations.
acceptance: A struct implementing Serialize + SchemaValidate can be published without additional ceremony
A struct implementing only Serialize (not SchemaValidate) cannot be passed to publish -- compile error
Subscribe's type parameter requires DeserializeOwned, not Deserialize<'a> with a lifetime
No method on ChannelHandle accepts raw bytes or untyped payloads

files: docs/design/sdk/briefs/SDK-002.json:69-86 — Requirement specifically tightens publish/subscribe bounds and bans untyped/raw-byte operations.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:67-111 — Existing raw-byte core publish methods are the anti-pattern the SDK trait must wrap/hide.
crates/liminal/src/channel/subscription.rs:27-40 — Existing subscription returns envelopes; SDK must deserialize into typed stream items instead of exposing envelope/raw payload APIs.
crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:79-109 — Runtime validation belongs behind SDK implementations using schema metadata, not as a caller-visible raw publish path.
Cargo.toml:17-23 — Clippy/panic/unwrap restrictions mean any compile-fail tests or examples should avoid lint bypasses and production `unwrap`/`expect`.
context: The important enforcement is at the method signature: `publish` and request side of `request_reply` must require `Serialize + SchemaValidate`; subscribe and reply side must require `DeserializeOwned`.

Do not add convenience methods such as `publish_bytes`, `publish_raw`, `subscribe_raw`, or `subscribe_envelopes` on `ChannelHandle`; even default helper methods can violate the acceptance if they expose untyped payloads.

`DeserializeOwned` should be imported from `serde::de`, not expressed as `for<'de> Deserialize<'de>` in public docs unless the acceptance wording is also satisfied by a type alias; using the named trait is clearer and avoids lifetime confusion.

Compile-time failure for non-`SchemaValidate` types is best verified with doctests using `compile_fail` or trybuild later; this brief may not require adding tests, but the public rustdoc should make the bound obvious.
scout.approach: When implementing R1, make R4 a signature review pass over `channel.rs`: `publish<M>(&self, message: M) -> Result<(), SdkError> where M: Serialize + SchemaValidate`; `subscribe<M>(&self) -> Result<Self::Subscription<M>, SdkError> where M: DeserializeOwned`; and `request_reply<Req, Resp>(&self, request: Req) -> Self::ReplyFuture<'_, Resp> where Req: Serialize + SchemaValidate, Resp: DeserializeOwned`. Keep serialization/schema metadata use entirely abstract in the trait; concrete embedded/remote implementations in SDK-004 can serialize or validate internally. In `types.rs`, do not provide any blanket implementation that makes all serializable types satisfy `SchemaValidate`, because that would make the negative acceptance impossible.
scout.notes: A subtle trap is using `async fn` in the trait via `async-trait`: it boxes futures and typically assumes std/send settings unless configured carefully, and generic async trait methods remain non-object-safe. Associated future types or explicit boxed future aliases under `alloc` make the non-object-safety tradeoff visible and keep no_std/WASM goals easier to preserve.

files_changed.note: Applied the required typed bounds and avoided any raw-byte or untyped publish/subscribe methods.
Kept `SchemaValidate` explicit with no blanket implementation for all serializable types.

dev.how: The enforcement is in the public method signatures: `publish<M>` and request `Req` require `Serialize + SchemaValidate`, while `subscribe<M>` and reply `Resp` require `serde::de::DeserializeOwned`. `ChannelHandle` contains no `publish_bytes`, `publish_raw`, envelope subscription, or untyped payload escape hatch. A `compile_fail` rustdoc example verifies that a merely serializable type cannot be published.

stories.note: A concrete handle implementing the trait can publish any user struct that implements `Serialize + SchemaValidate` without extra caller-side ceremony.

acceptance.criterion: A struct implementing Serialize + SchemaValidate can be published without additional ceremony
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:64-66 requires only M: Serialize + SchemaValidate for publish; crates/liminal-sdk/src/types.rs:68-71 defines the SchemaValidate trait user types implement.

acceptance.criterion: A struct implementing only Serialize (not SchemaValidate) cannot be passed to publish -- compile error
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:27-42 contains the only-Serialize compile_fail example; cargo test -p liminal-sdk reports that compile-fail doctest passed.

acceptance.criterion: Subscribe's type parameter requires DeserializeOwned, not Deserialize<'a> with a lifetime
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:45-47 and crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:72-74 require M: DeserializeOwned for Subscription<M> and subscribe<M>; no Deserialize<'a> lifetime-bound subscribe API exists.

acceptance.criterion: No method on ChannelHandle accepts raw bytes or untyped payloads
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:64-90 exposes only publish<M>, subscribe<M>, and request_reply<Req, Resp>; crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:13-16 explicitly documents that envelopes, byte buffers, protocol frames, and transport handles are not exposed.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement concrete channel or conversation types -- only trait definitions.
SHALL NOT implement connection management or transport logic.
SHALL NOT implement backpressure response types (deferred to SDK-003).
SHALL NOT implement embedded or remote mode distinction (deferred to SDK-004).
SHALL NOT add network dependencies to the crate.
verification: cargo check -p liminal-sdk compiles cleanly with trait definitions
cargo clippy -p liminal-sdk -- -D warnings passes with no diagnostics
cargo fmt -p liminal-sdk --check reports no formatting issues
All public traits and types have documentation comments
No file exceeds 500 lines of code
